name: British Columbians and our governments
description: The B.C. government is working with Indigenous Peoples to close socio-economic gaps.

name: Data and information management
description: The B.C. government and its agencies collect and create data. Datahelps detect trends, identify issues and develop better programs. Find out how you can access, explore and use government data and statistics.

name: Accessibility Statement
description: We are committed to making gov.bc.ca accessible and inclusive. Our goal is to meetLevel AA of the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG).

name: Employment, business and economic development
description: A targeted strategy to deliver major projects, expand skills training and grow key sectors to strengthen the economy.

name: Environmental protection and sustainability
description: Discover how B.C. is tackling climate change through pollution reduction, waste management and green innovation.

name: Taxes and tax credits
description: Stay up to date:Get email notificationswhen we update important information on our website.

name: Housing and tenancy
description: Tenants and landlords can find information and resources to guide them from the start of a tenancy to the end of a tenancy.

name: Law, crime and justice
description: Explore how justice is delivered in B.C., including mediation, arbitration, tribunals and system reform.

name: Public safety and emergency services
description: Learn about public safety in B.C. including emergency alerts, fire safety and protection services.

name: Careers & MyHR
description: The self-service website for prospective, new and existing BC Public Service employees to find information, answers and job search support.

name: Contact us
description: Service BC can guide you through Government of British Columbia programs and services.Information may be collectedto help us assess and respond to your enquiry.

name: Tourism and immigration
description: B.C.'s official travel and tourism site offers guides and tools to help you #ExploreBC.

name: Sports, recreation, arts and culture
description: Learn about sports participation, hosting, funding and safety. Find resources for athlete development.

name: Organizational structure
description: British Columbia's elected leaders and public servants are here to serve the citizens of B.C. and they're held accountable for their actions and decisions that impact residents. Learn about the different government bodies, the people that staff them and the work that they do.For more information and educational resources visit theParliamentary Education and Visitor Informationsite.

name: Lieutenant Governor
description: The Lieutenant Governor of B.C. represents the King at theprovincial level in Canada and is the legal head of state in British Columbia, which means he or she acts as chief public representative and has the highest ranking position in the provincial government.

name: Cabinet
description: The B.C. Executive Council, also called the Cabinet of British Columbia, includes the Lieutenant Governor in Council, the Premier, and Cabinet Ministers. These Ministers are members of the Legislative Assembly and are chosen by the Lieutenant Governor and Premier.

name: Office of the Premier
description: The Premier acts as the head of government for B.C. In other words, he or she is the first minister and chief officer of the executive branch.

name: Central Agencies
description: In addition to ministries and Crown corporations, there are a number of independent or partially independent agencies, boards and commissions that are essential to the effective governance of the province.

name: Ministries
description: The BC Public Service is divided into ministries. Each ministry is responsible for a specific area of public policy, government function or service delivery.

name: Legislative Assembly
description: The Legislature of British Columbia is composed of the Lieutenant Governor and 93 elected Members of the Legislative Assembly (MLAs). Together, members of the governing party, members of the opposition and independent members make up the B.C. Legislative Assembly.

name: Crown Corporations
description: Crown corporations are public sector organizations established and funded by the B.C. government to provide specialized goods and services to citizens. They operate at varying levels of government control and report on their planning, governance and accountabilities.

name: Education and training
description: Discover resources for early learning (birth to age 8) for parents, guardians, educators, and service providers.

name: Independent boards, commissions and tribunals
description: Independent boards, commissions and tribunals established by government to perform arbitration, regulation or a similar function.  The provincial government appoints at least one representative to these agencies.

name: Forms
description: Use this tool to find B.C. funding opportunities such as grants, bursaries and loans. Various opportunities are available for individuals, local governments, and community groups.

name: Services
description: Service BC provides front line support for many of the hundreds of programs and services the provincial government offers to residents, businesses and visitors.

name: Public Service
description: Members of the public service are the politically impartial government employees who are responsible for carrying out the day-to-day activities of government and for delivering public services to the citizens of B.C.

name: Financial disclosure
description: The intent of the Financial Disclosure Act is to identify what areas of influence and possible financial benefit an elected official, nominee or designated employee might have by virtue of their office, and to ensure the public has reasonable access to the information.

name: Driving and transportation
description: Cold temperatures, slippery roads, and reduced visibility challenge even the most experienced drivers. Learn winter driving safety tips.

name: B.C. Ministry Service Plans
description: In a world of uncertainty, Budget 2026 makes careful choices to protect what matters most and secure B.C.'s future.

name: Crown Agency Service Plans
description: In a world of uncertainty, Budget 2026 makes careful choices to protect what matters most and secure B.C.'s future.

name: Health
description: Find health care near you and learn how to apply for financial aid for medical-related travel.

name: Farming, natural resources and industry
description: Explore B.C.’s agriculture and seafood sectors. Find funding programs and resources for farms, businesses and agri-tech.

name: Family and social supports
description: Find support services for children, caregivers, parents and guardians. Access specialized resources to help your family.

name: Professional Credential Recognition
description: Many jobs in British Columbia are regulated.This means you need a certificate or license from a professional regulatory authorityto work in that occupation.

name: BC Gov News
description: Expanding access to advanced, specialized care for people throughout B.C.

name: About gov.bc.ca
description: Gov.bc.ca provides clear, easy, and secure access to public information and services for people in British Columbia.

name: Privacy
description: The British Columbia (B.C.) government is committed to protecting your privacy. The B.C. Government collects, uses and discloses your personal information in accordance with theFreedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act(FOIPPA)and other applicable legislation. ‘Personal information’ is defined broadly in the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act as recorded information about an identifiable individual, other than contact information, which is the information used to contact a person at a place of business. The purpose of this privacy statement is to inform you of the personal information that may be collected from you when visiting a B.C. government website and how that information may be used.

name: Disclaimer
description: This information is provided as a public service by the Government of British Columbia, Box 9411, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada V8W 9V1.
